Detail: Isohydric plants show stronger GPP-ET decoupling than anisohydric under drought
Δr = 0.032 (minimal, as predicted) — GPP-ET coupling remains stable under stress
Δr = 0.178 (strong, as predicted) — GPP-ET coupling breaks under stress
Both via hydraulic limitation: r(VPD,GPP|low-SWC) ≈ 0.32 (weak, rules out direct VPD)
69,725 daily records (50.4% retained after QC), 19 sites, 32.5k anisohydric, 37.1k isohydric
p < 0.001 for all correlations; effect size (Δr) differs by 5.6× between strategies
19 FLUXNET sites across 6 continents; pattern consistent within stomatal strategy groups
